text: The fifth generation (5G) of cellular wireless networks is evolving to deliver a significant performance improvement including higher data speeds, improved user quality-of-experience (QoE), very low latency and energy efficiency. In this paper, we give an overview of some key enabling technologies for 5G networks such as network densification, full-duplex, massive MIMO and cloud radio access network (C-RAN). In addition, we discuss the challenges associated with radio resource management for the 5G networks. We then elaborate on the measurement and test challenges for each 5G component and network feature.
